description: The quality of moving or being with effortless beauty and dignity. Grace encompasses physical elegance, spiritual blessing, and the Art Nouveau ideal of natural forms moving without strain or force.
rationale: The word begins with 'mi', a soft nasal that resonates from within, acknowledging that true grace springs from inner harmony. This flows through 'lo', creating the path of movement, the line that Art Nouveau celebrates—never forced, always flowing. The final 'a' opens this movement outward, letting grace breathe and expand into space. This three-syllable journey—from inner resonance through flowing movement to spacious presence—captures grace as both quality and action. The liquid sounds and open vowels make the word itself graceful, impossible to speak harshly or abruptly, teaching the mouth the very quality it names.
